ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for Tandoori Paneer Pidde. Mapped to 'flatbread food product' (FOODON_00002044) as the closest defensible ancestor class covering baked flatbread dishes.
ontology.foodex2_description: Pizza and similar flat bread products
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJW (Pizza and similar flat bread products) used as the closest defensible match for a baked flatbread topped with cheese and vegetables. No exact FoodEx2 entry exists for Tandoori Paneer Pidde.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this fusion flatbread dish. SNOMED CT coverage for prepared ethnic fusion dishes is limited.

main_ingredients: tandoori spice marinade (yogurt, cumin, coriander, turmeric, chili, ginger, garlic)

serving.natural_language_portion: One whole Veg Tandoori Paneer Pidde weighing approximately 350 g, equivalent to a single-serve oval flatbread topped with tandoori-marinated paneer and mixed vegetables.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Cheese, cottage, creamed (paneer proxy) and Pizza, cheese, frozen (flatbread topping proxy); ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods (paneer and spiced preparations)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service; National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN), India
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Veg Tandoori Paneer Pidde exists in any approved primary source. Nutrient values are estimated by component-level aggregation: wheat flatbread base from USDA FoodData Central (FDC ID 172892, pita bread), paneer from ICMR-NIN (paneer, 100g: protein ~18g, fat ~20g), tandoori spice marinade and vegetable toppings from USDA FoodData Central analogues. Input values (energy 237.5 kcal, protein 11.25g, fat 12.0g, carbohydrate 21.25g) are consistent with a mixed flatbread-cheese-vegetable dish and are retained with medium confidence. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, potassium, vitamins A/B-group, vitamin D, vitamin E, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, selenium, and water set to -1 as detailed values are not available in approved sources for this specific preparation.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; analogous estimate based on white pita bread / flatbread (GI ~72)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal (peer-reviewed)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Veg Tandoori Paneer Pidde. GI of 72 is estimated from white pita bread / wheat flatbread (GI ~72, University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care). The flatbread dough base is the dominant carbohydrate contributor. Paneer and vegetable toppings contribute protein and fat which may modestly reduce the effective GI of the composite dish; however, the base GI of 72 is used as a conservative estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (72 × 74.4) / 100 ≈ 53.6 (serving = 350g, carbohydrate = 21.25g/100g × 350g = 74.4g).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 450 mg/100g; a 350g serving delivers approximately 1,575 mg sodium, representing 68–79% of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2,000 mg.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to no more than 150g and request reduced-salt tandoori sauce and lower-sodium cheese to keep single-meal sodium below 600 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (~72) from refined wheat flatbread base with low dietary fibre (1.5g/100g); a 350g serving delivers ~74g carbohydrate with high glycemic load (~54), posing significant postprandial glucose elevation risk.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should restrict portion size to 100–150g, request a wholemeal or high-fibre dough alternative, and pair with a non-starchy vegetable side to blunt postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat at 6.25g/100g (a 350g serving provides ~21.9g saturated fat, exceeding the AHA daily limit of 13g) combined with sodium at 450mg/100g and trans fat at 0.13g/100g.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit this dish to occasional consumption, request reduced paneer and cheese quantities to lower saturated fat, and choose a preparation with olive oil rather than butter to improve the saturated-to-unsaturated fat ratio.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat 6.25g/100g and sodium 450mg/100g are pro-inflammatory; partially offset by anti-inflammatory turmeric (curcumin), ginger, garlic, and coriander in the tandoori spice blend.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis may benefit from the anti-inflammatory spice profile but should request reduced cheese and salt, and monitor symptoms if sensitive to nightshade vegetables (tomatoes, bell peppers).

llm_training.natural_language_description: Veg Tandoori Paneer Pidde is a vegetarian fusion flatbread dish served in Singapore, combining the Turkish Pidde (pide) — an oval or boat-shaped baked wheat flatbread — with an Indian tandoori-spiced paneer topping. The wheat dough is shaped into an elongated oval, generously topped with tandoori-marinated paneer (Indian fresh cheese), mixed vegetables including bell peppers, onions, and tomatoes, then baked in a high-temperature oven until the crust is crisp and the topping is cooked through. The result is a dish with a chewy, slightly crisp bread base, creamy and mildly spiced paneer, and charred vegetable notes from the tandoori marinade of yogurt, cumin, coriander, turmeric, chili, ginger, and garlic. Nutritionally, per 100g it provides approximately 237.5 kcal, 11.25g protein, 12.0g fat (6.25g saturated), 21.25g carbohydrate, 1.5g dietary fibre, and 450mg sodium. The dish is high in sodium and saturated fat, and carries a high glycemic index (~72) due to the refined wheat base, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension, type 2 diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. It is suitable for vegetarians and halal diets. The anti-inflammatory spice profile (turmeric, ginger, garlic) provides some nutritional benefit. Portion sizes sold in Singapore typically range from 350g (whole pidde) to 400g (medium pizza-style serving).

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Veg Tandoori Paneer Pidde exists in any approved primary nutritional database. Nutrient values are estimated by component-level aggregation using USDA FoodData Central (pita bread, paneer analogues) and ICMR-NIN (paneer). Input values from the GrabFood-sourced record are consistent with the estimated profile and have been retained. GI is estimated from the closest approved analogous food (white pita/flatbread, University of Sydney GI Database). Medium confidence reflects the estimation basis and absence of a direct approved-source match.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this specific Tandoori Paneer Pidde preparation are not available in USDA FoodData Central, ICMR-NIN, or Singapore HPB databases as of April 2026. 2. All core macros (protein_g 11.25, fat_g 12.0, carbohydrate_g 21.25) are non-zero and consistent with a mixed flatbread-cheese-vegetable dish; no zero-macro justification is required. 3. GI of 72 is an analogous estimate based on white pita bread / wheat flatbread from the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care); no direct GI measurement for this dish exists in approved sources. 4. The input record listed 'Veg Tandoori Panner Pidde' (misspelling of 'Paneer'); the canonical English name has been corrected to 'Veg Tandoori Paneer Pidde'. The original misspelling is retained as an alias for retrieval purposes. 5. Two default portions were listed in the input (400g and 350g); 350g (1 whole Pidde) is used as the primary serving size for GL calculation. 6. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean context (Chinese simplified/traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as this dish is sold in Singapore. 7. The dish is a Turkish-Indian fusion product sold via GrabFood in Singapore; country_of_origin is Singapore for this commercial preparation.
